recovery.name: Recovery & Sobriety
recovery.viewer_persona: A man in his late 30s to early 50s, in active recovery or struggling to stay sober. He is often awake at 2am not from cravings, but from the feelings sobriety uncovered that alcohol used to bury. He holds it together for his family. Nobody at work knows. He questions whether the flatness he feels will ever pass.

visual_moods.name: dawn_renewal
visual_moods.description: quiet early morning, new beginning, stillness before the world wakes

visual_moods.name: night_weight
visual_moods.description: late night solitude, weight of thoughts, darkness with single light source

visual_moods.name: forward_motion
visual_moods.description: movement, walking, journey, progress without destination

visual_moods.name: intimate_detail
visual_moods.description: close-up objects and hands carrying quiet emotional weight

ai_companion.name: AI Companion
ai_companion.viewer_persona: A man in his 40s who lives in his head. He has people around him but feels fundamentally alone like he is performing a version of himself rather than living. He is curious about technology. He would never call a helpline. He would talk to an AI before he would talk to a friend about how he really feels.

visual_moods.name: digital_solitude
visual_moods.description: person and technology at night, the glow of a screen as the only companion

visual_moods.name: reaching_connection
visual_moods.description: reaching toward light, bridge between isolation and connection

visual_moods.name: city_solitude
visual_moods.description: alone in a crowd, urban isolation, modern loneliness

visual_moods.name: abstract_light
visual_moods.description: abstract light and space as metaphor for technology and human connection

mens_mental_health.name: Men's Mental Health
mens_mental_health.viewer_persona: A man mid-30s to late 40s who has been the strong one for so long he has forgotten what it feels like to not be. He is not in crisis. He is just exhausted in a way he cannot explain to anyone. He would not use the word depressed. He would say he is not himself lately.

visual_moods.name: weight_of_silence
visual_moods.description: a man carrying something invisible, heavy still atmosphere

visual_moods.name: weather_mood
visual_moods.description: rain, grey skies, empty natural spaces as mood environment

visual_moods.name: empty_spaces
visual_moods.description: rooms and places that feel the absence of something

visual_moods.name: man_in_nature
visual_moods.description: man alone in vast natural environment, scale and solitude

mood_wellness.name: Mood & General Wellness
mood_wellness.viewer_persona: A man in his 30s to 40s who notices patterns in himself but has no framework to understand them. He is functional. He goes to the gym, does his job, looks fine from the outside. But something is off and he cannot name it. He is the type to download an app instead of talking to someone.

visual_moods.name: morning_ritual
visual_moods.description: intentional morning, routine, the calm start of something

visual_moods.name: reflection
visual_moods.description: mirrors, water, glass: moments of self-seeing

visual_moods.name: movement_wellness
visual_moods.description: body in motion, exercise, walking as mental health practice

visual_moods.name: minimal_calm
visual_moods.description: clean, minimal, quiet: order as a form of wellbeing
